final_response: Postmortem runbook complete: intermediates in tmp/incident-postmortem/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback/ (timeline.md, factors.md, actions.md), final at projects/postmortems/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback.md, three immediate action items promoted to followups.md, daybook entry written.
actions: Step 1: wrote tmp/incident-postmortem/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback/timeline.md with the four required headers (Detection, Diagnosis, Mitigation, Resolution).

Step 2: filled timeline.md with plausible incident events for 14:21-14:44 UTC (deploy of PR #4812, synthetic page at 14:29, ack 14:30, diagnosis to lazy-init bug in SessionTokenIssuer.refresh(), rollback 14:38, green 14:44).
Step 3: wrote factors.md with Contributing causes, Contributing context, Things that worked sections, reading from timeline.md.
Step 4: wrote actions.md with items tagged (immediate | next-quarter | aspiration), assigned to owners, reading from factors.md.
Step 5: wrote projects/postmortems/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback.md drawing sections from each intermediate and cross-referencing them.
Promoted three immediate action items to followups.md per runbook Notes.
Created daybook/2026-05-17.md with Intent, Log, Handoff sections per workspace discipline.
notes: Slug chosen: staging-deploy-rollback (incident time 14:30, staging, rollback).

Inputs invented per task instructions: incident attributed to PR #4812 refactor moving SessionTokenIssuer signing key from constructor injection to a lazy property; refresh-before-issue ordering returns null. No customer impact (staging only).
Followed the runbook's artifact-binding discipline: each step's output reads the prior step's artifact and the final composes from all three intermediates.

decisions.md and observations.md not amended — nothing in this incident rose to the bar of a future-constraining commitment or a durable observation worth keeping beyond the postmortem itself; the runbook says these are 'possibly' updated.
